text: The robotic sigmoidectomy procedure begins with the identification of the tumor located in the middle part of the sigmoid colon, followed by an extended lymph node dissection using the nose technique.
The surgeon initiates an extended lymph node dissection in the area surrounding the sigmoid colon, using robotic instruments to carefully separate and remove lymphatic tissue.

The surgeon performs skeletalization of the inferior mesenteric artery starting from the root, identifying the first sigmoid artery branching off the left colic artery. The artery is clipped and divided at its root.
The surgeon clips and divides the second sigmoid artery branching from the inferior mesenteric artery, followed by the initiation of a mesocolonectomy.

The surgeon mobilizes the bowel from the medial side in the interfacial space, progressing towards the left lateral canal. The distal line of resection is prepared, and the bowel is closed with a clip.
The surgeon washes, staples, and divides the lumen of the bowel. The posterior vaginal vault is opened, revealing the anatomical structures within the surgical field.

The surgeon delivers the anvil of the endostapler through the incision into the abdominal cavity. A colotomy is performed near the proximal line of resection, and the lumen of the gut is treated with antiseptic solution.
The surgeon installs a sleeve into the vaginal orifice, locates its lumen, and extracts the specimen through the vaginal opening.
The surgeon inserts the base of the circular stapler into the rectal stump to perform a coloanal side-to-end anastomosis, followed by the extraction of the stapler.
The surgeon verifies the integrity of the anastomosis rings and performs a bubble test to check for leaks. A drainage tube is installed, and the final view of the abdominal wall is shown.
